prompt: List the current workspace root and summarize the important directories and files for a coding agent.

context.task: List the current workspace root and summarize the important directories and files for a coding agent.
context.workspace_rule: Use only relative paths in the MCP workspace

read_file and extract project name, Python version, web framework, key dependencies, declared purpose","For start.py, mcp_server.py, login.py, start.sh: read_file with limit~60 to capture docstring/shebang/imports — confirm role (CLI launcher, FastAPI app, MCP server, auth flow)","For each top-level source directory (app, unified, worker, chatbai, skillboss, therouter, wavespeed, backup_gumloop_cli, docs): list_directory and capture top entries to infer role","Call file_info on 'ns' and on 'unifiedme' to determine whether they are CLI scripts, binaries, or stray files","Bucket every top-level entry into exactly one category: entry-points, config/deploy, source packages, instrumentation/interception, tests, utility scripts, documentation, runtime artifacts, environment/ignore","Compose the final summary: project name & stack (from README + requirements), main entry points (with paths), source-directory map (with observed contents), instrumentation cluster, docs cluster, tests cluster, ignore list — explicitly flag any item still not directly inspected"],"validation_checks":["Every top-level entry from list_directory('.') appears in exactly one plan.goal: Enumerate the workspace root and produce a coding-agent-oriented summary that buckets every top-level entry by role (entry points, config/deploy, source packages, instrumentation, tests, utility scripts, documentation, runtime artifacts, environment/ignore) with concrete evidence from file contents or directory listings. Read-only — no file mutations.
exploration_checks: Call list_directory('.') to capture every top-level entry with [DIR]/[FILE] markers and total count
Read project metadata files to confirm stack and purpose: read_file on README.md (limit ~80), requirements.txt (full), Dockerfile (full), VERSION (full)
Read entry-point candidates (head only, limit ~60): start.py, mcp_server.py, login.py, start.sh — capture shebang, module docstring, first imports
List every non-hidden top-level source directory once: list_directory on app/, unified/, worker/, chatbai/, skillboss/, therouter/, wavespeed/, backup_gumloop_cli/, docs/
Resolve extensionless root files via file_info on 'ns' and 'unifiedme' (size, type, executable bit)
Treat .git/, .venv/, .sisyphus/, .mcp_server.log, .gitignore as tooling/ignore — do NOT descend into them
execution_steps: Run list_directory('.') and record every entry
For README.md, requirements.txt, Dockerfile, VERSION: read_file and extract project name, Python version, web framework, key dependencies, declared purpose
For start.py, mcp_server.py, login.py, start.sh: read_file with limit~60 to capture docstring/shebang/imports — confirm role (CLI launcher, FastAPI app, MCP server, auth flow)
For each top-level source directory (app, unified, worker, chatbai, skillboss, therouter, wavespeed, backup_gumloop_cli, docs): list_directory and capture top entries to infer role
Call file_info on 'ns' and on 'unifiedme' to determine whether they are CLI scripts, binaries, or stray files

Bucket every top-level entry into exactly one category: entry-points, config/deploy, source packages, instrumentation/interception, tests, utility scripts, documentation, runtime artifacts, environment/ignore

Compose the final summary: project name & stack (from README + requirements), main entry points (with paths), source-directory map (with observed contents), instrumentation cluster, docs cluster, tests cluster, ignore list — explicitly flag any item still not directly inspected
validation_checks: Every top-level entry from list_directory('.') appears in exactly one bucket in the final summary
Each bucket assignment is backed by file contents read or directory listing observed — any item placed from filename alone is explicitly tagged 'inferred'
Project stack (language, framework, deploy target) is grounded in README.md + requirements.txt contents, not just filenames
'ns' and 'unifiedme' are resolved via file_info (size + type) or explicitly flagged as still unknown
Any subdirectory not list_directory'd is explicitly named as un-inspected — no fabricated role descriptions
Summary clearly distinguishes confirmed facts (read/listed) from inferred facts (named-only)

write_enabled is false — no write_file, edit_file, delete_file, rename_file, copy_file, create_directory, patch, download_file, or any mutating tool is invoked; only read_file, list_directory, file_info, glob_search, grep, tree, read_image are allowed

result.text:
1: # Unified AI Proxy
2: 
3: Single OpenAI-compatible endpoint that routes to multiple AI providers with account rotation, credit tracking, and centralized license management.
4: 
5: ## Providers
6: 
7: | Tier | Provider | Models |
8: |------|----------|--------|
9: | Standard | Kiro (AWS Q) | `claude-sonnet-4`, `deepseek-3.2`, `qwen3-coder-next` |
10: | Max | CodeBuddy | `claude-opus-4.6`, `gpt-5.4`, `gemini-2.5-pro` |
11: | WaveSpeed | WaveSpeed LLM | `new-claude-opus-4.7`, `new-gpt-5.2` |
12: | Max GL | Gumloop | `gl-claude-opus-4-7`, `gl-gpt-5.4` |
13: 
14: ## Quick Install
15: 
16: ```bash
17: curl -sSL https://unified-api.roubot71.workers.dev/install | bash
18: ```
19: 
20: Requirements: Python 3.10+, Git, curl.
21: 
22: ## Commands
23: 
24: ```bash
25: unifiedme run          # Start proxy (foreground, interactive)
26: unifiedme start        # Start proxy (background daemon)
27: unifiedme stop         # Stop background proxy
28: unifiedme status       # Show status, version, uptime
29: unifiedme update       # Pull latest code + reinstall deps
30: unifiedme kill-port    # Kill zombie process on port 1430
31: unifiedme logout       # Clear license key (switch license)
32: unifiedme help         # Show help
33: ```
34: 
35: ## First Time Setup
36: 
37: ```
38: 1. unifiedme run
39: 2. Enter your license key (UNIF-XXXXX-XXXXX-XXXXX-XXXXX-XXXXX)
40: 3. Open http://localhost:1430/dashboard
41: 4. Set your admin password
42: 5. Copy your API key (sk-xxx)
43: 6. Use in your IDE/CLI:
44:    Authorization: Bearer sk-xxx
45: ```
46: 
47: ## API Endpoints
48: 
49: ```
50: POST /v1/chat/completions    OpenAI-compatible chat (routes by model)
51: POST /v1/messages            Anthropic-compatible messages
52: GET  /v1/models              List available models
53: GET  /dashboard              Admin dashboard
54: ```
55: 
56: ## Architecture
57: 
58: ```
59: Client (IDE/CLI)
60:   |
61:   POST /v1/chat/completions
62:   Authorization: Bearer sk-xxx
63:   |
64: Unified Proxy (:1430)
65:   |-- Model Router (by model name -> tier)
66:   |-- Account Manager (sticky rotation, auto-ban)
67:   |-- Message Filter (find/replace before upstream)
68:   |-- Watchword Scanner (alert on keywords)
69:   |
70:   |-- Kiro Proxy     -> AWS Q API (direct Python)
71:   |-- CodeBuddy Proxy -> codebuddy.ai
72:   |-- WaveSpeed Proxy -> wavespeed.ai
73:   |-- Gumloop Proxy   -> WebSocket + Firebase auth
74:   |
75:   |-- License Client  -> Central D1 Database
76:       (sync accounts, usage logs, alerts every 5 min)
77: ```
78: 
79: ## License System
80:
